ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PES-25-308367, Estate of Bruna Loretta Bugeja; and
2) PTR-25-308415, The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust.

Appearances:
Patrick Sullivan for Robert Bugeja and Georgina Bugeja Spatola (via Zoom).
Donna Shioya for Ashley Reed (via Zoom).

The matters were called for status regarding mediation. The Court acknowledged receipt of the original pour-over will from Placer County and inquired whether the parties were prepared to proceed with mediation.

Mr. Sullivan reported that a petition to remove the trustee and for related relief had recently been filed and stated that mediation would be premature without clarification regarding trust assets and administration. Ms. Shioya responded that documents had been produced addressing the concerns raised and stated that mediation would still be appropriate.

The Court declined to order mediation at this time and directed counsel to meet and confer and discuss mediation with their clients. The matters were continued to April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) The Estate of Bruna Loretta Bugeja, PES-25-308367; and
2) The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust, PTR-25-308415.

Appearances (via Zoom):
Patrick Sullivan appeared for petitioners and proposed co-administrators Robert Bugeja and Georgina Bugeja Spatola.
Donna N. Shioya appeared for Ashley Reed, Respondent/Petitioner/Trustee.

Ms. Shioya reported that the order transferring venue to San Francisco had been submitted but had not yet been signed. The Court inquired about the parties' interest in mediation and the anticipated course of the litigation.

The Court inquired about mediation and the anticipated course of the litigation. Both sides indicated openness to mediation but stated that further discussion with their respective clients was needed before mediation could be productive, given the procedural posture of the cases and ongoing discovery.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to March 2, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set a status hearing regarding mediation for the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Continued To Dec-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Continued To Dec-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PES-25-308367, Estate of Bruna Loretta Bugeja; and
2) PTR-25-308415, The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust.

Appearances:
Patrick Sullivan for proposed co-administrators Robert Bugeja and Georgina Bugeja Spatola (via CourtCall).
Donna Shioya for trustee Ashley Reed (via Zoom).

Ms. Shioya reported that an ex parte petition to transfer the will from Placer County to San Francisco had been filed but was still pending.

The Court suggested continuing the matter to allow time for a response to the will contest to be filed and for proper service of summons. Ms. Shioya brought to the Court's attention that a petition filed by Mr. Sullivan appeared to be missing page 3 - both on the copy she received as well as the copy available on the Court's website. Mr. Sullivan was directed to amend a petition to include the missing 3rd page prior to the next hearing.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PES-25-308367, Estate of Bruna Loretta Bugeja; and
2) PTR-25-308415, The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust.

Appearances:
Patrick Sullivan for co-administrators Robert Bugeja and Georgina Bugeja Spatola (via Zoom).
Donna N. Shioya for trustee Ashley Reed (via Zoom).

A discussion was held regarding the competing petitions currently at issue. Mr. Sullivan confirmed that written objections to the pour-over will were filed earlier in the week. Ms. Shioya stated that the pour-over will had been lodged in Placer County and that an ex parte petition will be required to transfer the will to San Francisco Superior Court pursuant to Probate Code section 8203.

Mr. Sullivan requested that an independent administrator be appointed. The Court advised that such relief requires a separate petition, with identification of the proposed administrator and written consent from that person. The Court explained that San Francisco does not appoint neutrals but requires the parties to propose candidates.

The Court noted that a motion to transfer venue in the trust matter is already scheduled for September 10, 2025. Given the filings and pending motion, the Court determined the appropriate course was to continue both matters.

The Court continued both matters to October 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
